claim: Cal.com offers unlimited free scheduling for individual users
Cal.com provides a public API and developer documentation for building integrations
Cal.com supports OAuth client integration for third‑party apps
Cal.com includes built‑in video conferencing via its Cal Video platform
Cal.com integrates with Stripe to accept payments for bookings
Cal.com offers webhooks to notify external systems of scheduling events
Cal.com provides AI‑powered scheduling features under the Cal.ai brand
Cal.com targets teams of all sizes, offering solutions for individuals, teams, organizations, and enterprises
Cal.com allows setting minimum notice (24 hours) and buffers (30 minutes before and after events)
Cal.com offers customizable booking links (e.g., cal.com/cedric)
Cal.com offers a 14-day free trial for its scheduling software.
Cal.com provides round robin scheduling that distributes meetings among team members based on weights, priorities, or the person least recently booked.
Cal.com supports dynamic group links that allow creating group meetings on-the-fly by adding a "+" between usernames.
Cal.com can accept payments for bookings through Stripe or PayPal integrations.
Cal.com offers an API, OAuth client creation, and webhooks for developers to build integrations.
Cal.com provides automated workflow features such as meeting reminders and follow‑ups.
Cal.com includes lead routing software that directs bookers to the right person based on details like industry or company size.
Cal.com advertises that it is trusted by fast‑growing companies around the world.
Cal.com offers a dedicated app store that integrates with existing tools and apps.
Cal.com provides a feature called "Collective Events" that lets users schedule meetings with several team members at once.

Cal.com offers an Individuals plan that is free forever for a single user with unlimited event types and calendars, email & SMS notifications, integration with 100+ apps, mobile app, browser extension, Stripe & PayPal payments, two-way Salesforce & HubSpot sync, and 1-click Calendly import.
Cal.com offers a Teams plan priced at $12 per month per user on a yearly subscription, representing a 25% discount.

The Teams plan includes scheduling across one team, round-robin scheduling, managed & collective event types, recurring events, customizable email & SMS notifications, removal of Cal.com branding, routing forms, booking analytics, and custom APIs.
Cal.com offers an Organizations plan priced at $28 per month per user on a yearly subscription, representing a 25% discount.

The Organizations plan adds unlimited sub-teams, routing by custom variables, a company subdomain, SAML SSO and SCIM, SOC 2, HIPAA, ISO 27001 compliance, instant meetings, domain‑wide delegation, role‑based permissions, and additional APIs.

Cal.com provides an Enterprise Custom plan with no published price; interested customers must talk to sales for pricing and receive dedicated onboarding, engineering support, SLA & uptime guarantees, HRIS & directory integrations, priority Slack & account support, and a dedicated database.
Both the Teams and Organizations plans offer a 14‑day free trial.
Cal.com provides scheduling tools specifically for talent acquisition teams to coordinate interviews and streamline recruitment workflows.
The platform supports round robin scheduling that distributes meetings among team members based on weights, priorities, or the person least recently booked.
Cal.com enables collective events, allowing a meeting to be booked with several team members at the same time.
Routing forms in Cal.com can direct bookers to the appropriate recruiter based on details such as industry or company size.
Dynamic meetings automatically show availability across multiple time zones for all participants.
Automated workflows in Cal.com can send meeting reminders and other notifications without manual effort.
Cal.com integrates with Stripe and PayPal to process payments and place holds for bookings.
The platform offers an app store that syncs with existing tools and applications used by recruiting teams.
Cal.com allows custom fields on booking forms to collect candidate-specific information such as position applied for or resume uploads.
The service can handle multi-step interview processes, coordinating availability for phone screens, technical rounds, and panel interviews.
Cal.com automatically adjusts for different time zones, facilitating scheduling with international candidates.
Group bookings can include multiple interviewers in a single session.
Cal.com is marketed as a tool to accelerate hiring by reducing back‑and‑forth emails and improving candidate experience.
Collective Events let users with team plans in the same organization book meetings with multiple coworkers by typing a Cal.com URL and adding a plus sign before each coworker’s username.
Cal.com automatically cross‑references the calendars of all participants when a collective event is created.
Collective Events are marketed as a feature for teams and enterprises to simplify scheduling across multiple participants.
Cal.com’s mission is to connect a billion people by 2031 through calendar scheduling.
The feature is described as “easy” and “smarter, simpler scheduling” and is promoted with a “Get started” call‑to‑action.
Cal.com supports round-robin scheduling that distributes meetings among team members based on weights, priorities, or the person least recently booked.
Cal.com enables collective events, allowing a meeting to be booked with several team members at the same time.
Cal.com integrates with popular CRMs such as Salesforce and HubSpot.
Cal.com offers white‑label solutions so booking pages can be branded to match a company’s look and feel.
Custom fields can be added to booking forms to collect information like company name, job title, or specific interests.
Cal.com automatically adjusts for different time zones, ensuring accurate scheduling for international clients.
Payments for bookings can be processed through Stripe or PayPal integrations.
Cal.com provides a public API and developer documentation for building custom integrations.
Cal.com offers automated workflows such as meeting reminders and routing forms that direct bookers to the right team member based on details like industry or company size.
Cal.com integrates with video conferencing tools and supports instant scheduling.

The primary programming languages used across the calcom GitHub organization are JavaScript, Python, Shell, and TypeScript.

verdict.bull: Strong open-source traction with 45k+ GitHub stars, comprehensive feature set across tiers, and compliance certifications (SOC2, HIPAA) that appeal to enterprises.
verdict.bear: No disclosed customer numbers, revenue, or market share; thin market facts leave growth and competitive positioning unclear.

risk_matrix.reason: Product offering is comprehensive with clear tiers and features, but no data on customer traction, revenue, or market share.
Active open-source development with high community engagement, but the main repo has over 1,300 open issues, indicating potential maintenance challenges.
Limited market data available; only marketing claims without evidence of market adoption, competitive positioning, or revenue growth.

section_summaries.overview: Cal.com offers a tiered scheduling platform from a free individual plan to enterprise custom, with features like unlimited event types, CRM integrations, payments, and compliance certifications.
section_summaries.engineering: The platform is built on open-source TypeScript and Python, with active GitHub repositories (45k+ stars) supporting round-robin, collective events, time zone handling, and a public API.
section_summaries.market: Cal.com markets itself as a scheduling solution for teams of all sizes, with a specific emphasis on recruitment use cases, though broader market traction data is not provided.
